questions.text: The order of a differential equation is the order of the:
questions.explanation: The order of a differential equation is the order of the highest order derivative present.

questions.text: The degree of a differential equation is the power of the highest order derivative.
questions.explanation: The degree is the power of the highest order derivative when the equation is polynomial in derivatives.

questions.text: A differential equation of order 1 and degree 1 is called ________.
questions.explanation: A differential equation with order 1 and degree 1 is called first order first degree differential equation.

questions.text: What is a particular solution of a differential equation?
questions.correctAnswer: A solution obtained by giving specific values to arbitrary constants in the general solution is called particular solution.
questions.explanation: General solution contains arbitrary constants. Particular solution is obtained by assigning specific values to these constants.
